subject: [PATCH 2/3] iomap: decouple simple direct I/O reads from iomap_dio_rw

reviews.author: changfengnan
reviews.summary: Reviewer changfengnan noted that the inode_dio_end() call in the synchronization scenario is missing a goto label to properly handle errors, and suggested adding a goto out_dio_end label before calling inode_dio_end().

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the new function __iomap_dio_simple() is not strictly limited to reads and suggested making its name more consistent by adding _read to it, similar to other functions in the implementation.

Reviewer noted that the patch was inadvertently sent to a limited audience instead of the intended recipients, causing confusion about its visibility.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah.  I wanted the read out of the guts of the implementation, as
most of the code is not strictly limited to reads.  But with the public
API needing it, we might as well be consistent in the function naming.

---

Yes.  Sashiko also reported that yesterday and I replied with a patch,
but I now noticed the report and thus my reply only went to me,
a sashiko-review list and Ted, but not everone else or the lists.

WTF?

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the synchronous error handling in inode_dio_end() needs to be done by __iomap_dio_simple(), as it properly accounts for the synchronous case and calls iomap_dio_simple_complete() which then calls inode_dio_end() even on errors.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, minus the label naming that is exactly what I came up with
yesterday and it has survived testing over night.  Looks like xfstests
currently never triggers the sychronous error return path, I'll see
if I can increase coverage for that.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch decouples simple direct I/O reads from the iomap_dio_rw function, making it cleaner and more modular. It introduces a new function __iomap_dio_simple() to handle these reads, which properly accounts for synchronous cases by calling inode_dio_end() even on errors.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch decouples simple direct I/O reads from the iomap_dio_rw function, making it cleaner and more modular. It introduces a new function __iomap_dio_simple() to handle these reads, which properly accounts for synchronous cases by calling inode_dio_end() even on errors.
